summary: Scorching Scarlet>Fire, soaring Eyes of the World, tight second‑set jam
blurb: The show erupts with a blistering Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain and a soaring Eyes Of The World. Jerry’s lead work is on fire while Brent adds lush keyboard textures throughout. Overall a high‑energy performance that shines especially in the second set.
review: April 3, 1982 at The Scope captured one of the spring‑’82 tour’s brightest moments. From the opening Alabama Getaway through a solid first set, the band built momentum that exploded with the iconic Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain segue, followed by an inspired Estimated Prophet into Eyes Of The World. Reviewers rave about Jerry’s shredding solos and vocal fire on Scarlet>Fire, Brent’s piano‑driven interplay on the Estimated→Eyes outro, and the relentless groove laid down by Mickey and Bill on Not Fade Away. Bob Weir’s rhythm work on Me And My Uncle and C.C. Rider adds extra drive, while Phil’s bass remains rock‑solid despite a few recording‑level limitations. The second set stays tight and jazzy, delivering a beautiful Stella Blue and a rousing encore of U.S. Blues. With multiple soundboard and audience sources agreeing on the show’s high energy and musical peaks, this performance stands out as a must‑listen snapshot of the band in top form.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Across all recordings the sentiment is overwhelmingly positive: multiple reviewers call the Scarlet>Fire transition "blazing" or "scorching", praise Jerry’s guitar and vocal work, highlight Brent’s keyboard contributions, and consistently note the high‑energy second set. No recurring negative comments about performance quality were found, just mixed remarks on recording fidelity, leading to a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard transfer, crisp and off‑the‑charts audio that captures the entire second‑set firestorm with no audible flaws.

key_highlights: Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain segue ignites the night.
Eyes Of The World delivers a soaring, interstellar jam in the second set.
Brent’s piano work shines on Estimated Prophet → Eyes and Never Trust A Woman.
Jerry’s guitar shredding throughout, especially on Scarlet>Fire.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers scorching guitar solos and fiery vocals, especially on Scarlet Begonias → Fire; noted for a possible instrument swap that added extra groove.
band_performance.Phil: Provides solid, lock‑in bass foundation throughout; side‑swap excitement is mentioned but his playing remains dependable despite some recording limitations.
band_performance.Bob: Ripping rhythm guitar work drives the set, highlighted on Me And My Uncle and C.C. Rider, adding power to the band's overall thrust.
band_performance.Brent: Focused grand‑piano and keyboard textures stand out on Estimated Prophet → Eyes and Never Trust A Woman, contributing lush harmonic depth.
band_performance.Mickey: Energetic drumming propels Not Fade Away and the second‑set jams; praised for maintaining a relentless groove.
band_performance.Billy: Pairs with Mickey to create tight, dynamic percussion throughout, especially effective during the high‑energy sections of the show.
